name: Первозданный Стрелок
description: In the areas of Etharis where nature still holds sway, many cling to the ancient customs of woodland hunters and witches. Marking themselves with wild plants and bone needles, theirs is a tradition of cunning, cruelty, and reprisal against all trespassers. Unlike the Druids, these wood witches twist natural magic into hexes, imbuing their weapons with nature's fury.
shortDescription: Channel the Wrath of the Wilds

features.name: Elemental Arrows
features.description: As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue a Longbow or Shortbow with elemental energy for 1 minute. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. For the duration, the imbued weapon deals damage of the selected type instead of its normal type and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the chosen type when it hits. At the start of each of your turns, you can change this choice. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Herbal Lore
features.description: You gain an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} and are proficient with it. You can use the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to stabilize an Unconscious creature within 5 feet of you that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as if using a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} without needing to make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check. If you take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, an Unconscious creature within 5 feet of you that has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} gains 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Primordial Archer Spells
features.description: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Primordial Archer Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Weave the Elements
features.description: With 1 hour of work or when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can use an {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} to mark yourself with elemental patterns. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

features.name: Witching Arrows
features.description: You gain the ability to imbue curses into your arrows. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a ranged attack using a Longbow or Shortbow, you can expend a level 1+ spell slot to choose one of the following effects (no action required). The listed damage increases by {@damage 2d6} for each spell slot above 1.

details.arcingShot: {@b Arcing Shot.} Electricity crackles around the arrow. Make a ranged attack roll with the same weapon against a second creature within 30 feet of the first that is also within your range. Each creature hit takes {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage.
details.entanglingShot: {@b Entangling Shot.} The wooden shaft of the arrow sprouts tiny green leaves. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes the damage only. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
details.hexingShot: {@b Hexing Shot.} The magic surrounding your arrow clouds the mind of your target. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage and gains the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute (your choice). The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.
details.viperShot: {@b Viper Shot.} The arrow transforms into a hissing serpent. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Poison damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.

features.name: Primordial Magic
features.description: Taking damage can't break your {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB} on any Ranger spells you cast. In addition, you can take a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} to change the damage type you chose for the Weave the Elements feature to a different damage type in the list. When you do, you can choose a creature that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} damage of either damage type (your choice). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
